title: cathartolinum neomexicanum
intro: Cathartolinum neomexicanum is a plant species, also known as Mexican buckthorn or cathartol, which is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation due to its anthraquinone content. The plant's anthraquinones have been shown to have health benefits such as promoting digestive regularity and potentially reducing the risk of certain gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, cathartolinum neomexicanum is often used as a laxative or in combination with other herbs to support digestive health, but it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects.
